description: Our Durga Puja pandal this year was a replica of the Chennakeshava temple complex at Belur, Karnataka. Belur was an early capital of the Hoysala Empire and one of the finest examples of Hoysala architecture. The Chennakeshava Temple, built by King Vishnuvardhana in 1117 CE to commemorate his victory over the Cholas at Talakad, is renowned for its intricate friezes of elephants, lions, horses, episodes from the epics and the celebrated Shilabalikas (celestial dancers), including the famed Darpana Sundari. The temple — proposed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site — inspired the detailed craftsmanship of our pandal.
The festival continued to draw lakhs of visitors from Siliguri and across North Bengal, earning a string of honours for the pandal, lighting, idol and greenery.
awards: Best District Puja — Oscar News Time
Best Pratima (Sarod Samman) — Kolkata Weir Industries Ltd. (North Bengal Division)
Prize for Best Greenery — Siliguri Horticulture Society
Award for cleanliness and pollution-free environment — Siliguri Municipal Corporation
Award for Social Messages — Siliguri Municipal Corporation

theme: Cultural Heritage of the States of India
description: With growing provincialism among people, a unique theme was organised where the multi-coloured cultural heritage of each state of our nation was presented in 28 separate blocks for individual states. Almost all of India's heritage monuments were recreated for exhibition, giving visitors extra knowledge of the country's cultural and historical richness.
awards: 1st Prize (North Bengal) — Press Club of North Bengal
3rd Prize — Decorators Association of North Bengal

description: The 2009 celebration was based on our ancient Indian culture. Cave No. 16 of Ellora — the Kailasa temple — was recreated for the public, depicting sculptures of Hindu deities of that ancient period. A large waterfall was built with water running from the top of the cave, enjoyed by lakhs of visitors.

The festival was celebrated with a multi-coloured exposition of ancient Egyptian culture over a total area of 75,000 sq. ft., winning a good number of dignified awards across Siliguri and North Bengal.

theme: Silver Jubilee — Replica of the frontal of "Petit Palace", France
description: Being the Silver Jubilee year, the festival was celebrated in an extravagant manner. A replica of the frontal part of the "Petit Palace" of France was exposed across a span of 60,000 sq. ft. to the people of North Bengal, winning many prestigious awards from Siliguri as well as Kolkata. It was appreciated by everyone who witnessed the festival.
